daily_brief.headline: Risk-off: 50% breadth, tighten stops; macro neutral

highlights.title: Sensex today | Stock Market Highlights: Sensex plunges 1,456 pts, Nifty closes at 23,379, Rupee falls to 95.63/$ on Iran-US deadlock
Sensex dips 3,400 points in 4 days. Is this the beginning of a bigger crash?
Euro zone bond yields jump as Middle East peace deal hopes fade
Global Markets | Australian shares fall for third day as banks weigh ahead of federal budget
HSBC slashes FY27 GDP estimate sharply to 6%, expects two RBI rate hikes
Modi says Iran war poses severe risks to India, urges cuts in fuel use and gold purchases

daily_brief.key_insight: Risk-off regime with 50% breadth and median momentum -1.7%. Raise cash, honour stops, resist bottom-fishing until breadth recovers above 55%. Macro flow is neutral with 172 policy/global items in focus — factor this into position sizing. Sector dispersion is wide: Oil & Gas leads at 60 while Electronics lags at 0 — a stock-picker's tape.

conviction_board.name: Apollo Hospitals
conviction_board.narrative: Here's the wrinkle: the trend is intact but momentum has started to cool. That usually means consolidation ahead, not a collapse — but chasing here is risky. The trend is solidly positive — price sits above all key averages, though alignment isn't perfect. Momentum is flat — RSI at 68 sits in no-man's land. The stock is waiting for a trigger. Market headwinds are real — 50% breadth in a bearish regime means even good setups get sold. Bull case: sustains above ₹8,022 and pushes towards ₹8,352 as trend followers pile in. Bear case: a slip below ₹7,693 triggers trailing stops and dents the short-term thesis. Needs to resolve ₹7,782–₹8,215 before a directional bet pays. Verdict: HOLD. Own it? Let it work. Don't own it? Cleaner entries exist elsewhere. Outlook: Neutral → Neutral → Neutral.

Market headwinds are real — 50% breadth in a bearish regime means even good setups get sold. FMCG is lagging the broader tape (score 17) — an extra headwind.
